title: Falafel Wrap with Tahini

seo.title: Falafel Wrap Recipe: Easy Middle Eastern Lunch
seo.metaDescription: Craving a delicious Middle Eastern lunch? Learn how to make the best falafel wrap with creamy tahini sauce at home. This easy recipe is perfect for a quick and healthy meal.
longTailKeywords: how to make Falafel Wrap with Tahini
easy Falafel Wrap with Tahini recipe
authentic Middle Eastern Falafel Wrap with Tahini
Falafel Wrap with Tahini step by step
best Falafel Wrap with Tahini at home
lowCompetitionKeywords: homemade Falafel Wrap with Tahini recipe
Middle Eastern lunch Falafel Wrap with Tahini
seo.h1: Easy Falafel Wrap with Tahini Recipe
h2Headings: What is Falafel Wrap with Tahini?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Delicious homemade falafel wrap with fresh vegetables and creamy tahini sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of the Middle East with this vibrant and flavorful falafel wrap, drizzled with homemade tahini.

description: Savor the flavors of the Middle East with this vibrant Falafel Wrap, a perfect blend of crispy falafel, fresh vegetables, and creamy tahini sauce. This satisfying lunch offers a delightful texture and taste experience, bringing authentic street food right to your kitchen.

instructions.title: Prepare the Falafel Mixture
instructions.text: In a food processor, combine the drained chickpeas, chopped onion, garlic cloves, fresh parsley, fresh cilantro, ground cumin, ground coriander, flour, baking powder, salt, and black pepper. Pulse until the mixture is finely chopped but not pureed. It should be coarse enough to hold its shape. Transfer the mixture to a bowl, cover, and refrigerate for at least 15 minutes to firm up.

instructions.title: Form and Fry the Falafel
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven to 350°F (175°C). Using a falafel scoop or your hands, form the chickpea mixture into small patties or balls, about 1-1.5 inches in diameter. Carefully drop the falafel into the hot oil, frying in batches to avoid overcrowding. Fry for 3-5 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown and crispy on all sides. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.

instructions.title: Make the Tahini Sauce
instructions.text: While the falafel is frying, prepare the tahini sauce. In a small bowl, whisk together the tahini, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, and a pinch of salt. Gradually add cold water, whisking continuously, until the sauce reaches a smooth, creamy, and pourable consistency. You may need more or less water depending on your desired thickness.

instructions.title: Prepare the Vegetables
instructions.text: Dice the tomatoes and cucumbers, and shred the lettuce. If using, slice the pickled turnips or pickles. Arrange all the prepared vegetables and falafel on a platter for easy assembly.

instructions.title: Warm the Pita Bread
instructions.text: Lightly warm the pita bread or flatbreads in a dry skillet over medium heat for about 30 seconds per side, in the microwave for 15-20 seconds, or briefly in the oven. This makes them more pliable and easier to wrap.

instructions.title: Assemble the Wraps
instructions.text: Lay a warm pita bread flat. Spread a generous amount of tahini sauce down the center. Arrange 3-4 falafel patties on top of the sauce, then add a handful of shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, diced cucumbers, and sliced pickled turnips (if using). Drizzle with more tahini sauce if desired. Fold in the sides of the pita and then roll it up tightly to form a wrap. Serve immediately and enjoy your homemade falafel wrap!

tips: For extra crispy falafel, ensure your oil is at the correct temperature (350°F/175°C) and do not overcrowd the pot, which can lower the oil temperature.
If you prefer to bake your falafel for a lighter option, flatten the patties slightly and bake at 375°F (190°C) for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden.
The tahini sauce can be made ahead of time and stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. You might need to add a splash of water and re-whisk before serving.
article: Falafel Wrap with Tahini is a beloved Middle Eastern Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
